---
title: "LRP95: Mechanism model — letter-sound (LS) -> word reading (WR), moderated by taught-expressive vocabulary (TE)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP95 **completes** the letter-sound × vocabulary interaction set —
[LRP71](../lrp-rli-mech-071) ($E$), LRP93 ($R$), LRP94 ($TR$), LRP95 ($TE$) — so the
joint-readiness question ("do letter sounds and vocabulary have to be high together for larger
word-reading gains?") is answered for all four vocabulary measures. It extends the
[LRP58](../lrp-rli-mech-058) letter-sound -> word-reading curve $f^L$ with a **linear moderation**
by **taught-expressive** vocabulary $TE$.

Letter sounds enter as the nonparametric **HSGP** curve $f^L$; taught-expressive vocabulary enters
as a standardised linear main effect plus the interaction:

$$
\eta = \dots + f^{L}(\operatorname{logit} L_{\text{post}})
       + \gamma_{\text{mod}}\, z(TE)
       + \gamma_{\text{int}}\, z(\operatorname{logit} L_{\text{post}})\, z(TE)
$$

with the regularising $\mathrm{Normal}(0, 0.3)$ cross-coupling prior on both coefficients.

_Reading the interaction._ $\gamma_{\text{int}}$ is the change in the letter-sound -> word-reading slope per +1 SD of
taught-expressive vocabulary (and, by symmetry, the change in the vocabulary slope per +1 SD of
letter sounds).

- $\gamma_{\text{int}} > 0$ = **synergy** (both high needed).
- $\gamma_{\text{int}} \approx 0$ = **additive** (either helps, roughly independently).
- $\gamma_{\text{int}} < 0$ = **substitutive** (diminishing returns to having both).

Because $z(\operatorname{logit} L)$ is centred, $\gamma_{\text{mod}}$ is the $TE$ main effect at the
mean of letter sounds and $f^L$ is the $L \to W$ effect at the mean of $TE$. $L$ and $TE$ are
positively correlated in this cohort, so the discordant corners are sparse and the interaction is
**weakly powered** — exploratory only.

## Adjustment set

The revised LRP58 $L \to W$ set (#245): $\{G, A, HS, IS(\text{attend}), SP, W_{\text{pre}}\}$. The
moderator $TE$ enters **additionally**, via its main effect and interaction only (same construction
as LRP71).

_TE enters as the tested moderator only._ Conditioning on the vocabulary moderator can open a collider path for the $L$ main association, so
the $L$ main slope is read **only with** the moderation.

_Adjusted association, not causal._ Latent general ability ($GA$) is not adjusted for, so $f^L$, $\gamma_{\text{mod}}$ and
$\gamma_{\text{int}}$ are **latent-GA-confounded adjusted associations**, never causal effects. The
randomised causal claim lives in the ITT suite.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_mechanism.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
